• 🎯 Management expects the platform to help enterprises train larger AI models and deploy production-grade AI applications more efficiently

Scoda Tubes Concoll Highlights

1. Domestic revenue mix declined from ~73% to ~65%, while exports increased from 26% to ~35%, showing stronger international demand and diversification.
2. Company is targeting 25% Revenue growth of conservative basis and margins of 14-15%
3. Piercing plant shutdown due to elevated gas prices impacted operations for roughly 15–17 days which impacted topline by around 20 odd crores.  Starting April the plant commenced operations.
4. Operations affected as they use PNG which is largely imported through Qatar.The prices are still impacting and have risen by 25-30%. 
5. Welded tubes are seeing emerging opportunities in data centres, water treatment, construction and HVAC, which management highlighted as future growth sectors.No orders from Data center yet but management will target a few this year. 
6. Seamless pipe expansion of 10,000 MTPA is now operational and now total capacity is 20,000MTPA.
7. Welded pipe capacity expansion is expected to be commissioned in H2 FY27, with trials likely in Q2 FY27 with a capacity of 8,000MTPA and optimal contribution from FY28 
8. FY26 capex planned at around ₹110 crore and FY27 capex estimated at around ₹41 crore.
9. Of the ₹220 crore IPO proceeds, approximately:
    ₹74 crore remains as bank balance,
     ₹50 crore allocated towards working capital,
    ₹27 crore towards capex.

Peak debt next year could reach around ₹250 crore due to ongoing expansion.

Inventory days have risen to 217 days due to higher gas prices and raw material stocking; management expects this to normalize towards 160 days.

Primary raw material is stainless steel round bars produced from scrap, where prices have increased by 25–30%.

Hollow mill backward integration progressing; management targets 100% captive consumption by FY29 (currently ~70%).

Solar power project of 8.79 MW DC (4.99 MW for seamless, 3.8 MW for welded division) expected to generate ~137 lakh kwh units annually and save roughly ₹8.6 crore in electricity costs.

The company is in the final stages of obtaining Marine approval, which would open opportunities in the Italian marine market, expected within 3–4 weeks.

Welded they palnned to do up to 8 inches, but suppliers wanted upto 16 inches, so they  now they are increasing it to 16 inches 

Capacity for seamless  would be 70% and  welded to be  25% FY’27 (noted: on 13K capacity)

BHEL tender is delayed till July/Aug from Nov last year.

reports.text:
Deepinder Goyal tells FT that quick commerce rivals are operating in "burn-to-earn" mode, while Eternal's Blinkit has a 50% market share:

"We are profitable at 50 per cent of market share. The rest of the industry is in a burn-to-earn mode, where they are spending $2 bn for $5-6 bn dollars worth of NOV."

Also, Goyal on competition from Amazon in quick commerce:

"If it comes to outspending Amazon, we can......We have seen them compete in food delivery with us. They’ve shut it down. They’re not going to let go of quick commerce so easily. But we still think that we have got a better shot at winning."

RATEGAIN RELEASES APMEA HOTEL BOOKING REPORT; FLAGS AI SEARCH DISRUPTION 🤖🏨

• 📊 RateGain launched the APMEA Direct Booking Friction Report 2026, covering hotel booking journeys across the region

• 🔍 Report warns that AI-powered search is changing customer discovery patterns and could push hotel websites out of the booking journey

• 👀 42% of hotels do not appear on Page 1 for unbranded search queries, reducing visibility to potential guests

• 💰 63% of hotels offer higher rates on their own websites than OTAs, leading to loss of direct bookings

• ⚡ 72% of hotel websites fail the 3-second load benchmark; average mobile load time stands at 4.1 seconds

• 🛒 Guests require an average of 4 clicks to complete a booking versus a global benchmark of 3 clicks

• 🔒 44% of hotels disclose taxes and fees only at the final payment stage, impacting customer trust and conversions

• 🌏 Singapore and Thailand emerged as regional leaders in website speed and direct booking experience

• 📈 RateGain highlighted that hotels moving to unified digital platforms have seen up to 5x growth in direct revenue within 90 days

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strengthens RateGain's positioning as an AI-powered travel technology provider while highlighting the growing need for its digital marketing, distribution and direct booking optimization solutions.)

reports.text:
COAL INDIA

Coal India's coal gasification push may see ₹60,000 crore investment ; REPORTS 

Coal India's three planned gasification projects could attract investments of up to 60,000 crore as India pushes cleaner coal utilisation

Ashok Leyland: Guidance - Concall Update

Management expressed cautious optimism for FY27, anticipating positive demand drivers for commercial vehicles but acknowledging macroeconomic headwinds like global economic uncertainties, commodity price volatility, and diesel price increases.

FY27 capital expenditure is projected to be between INR750 crores and INR1,000 crores.

Investments in subsidiaries will be need-based, with Hinduja Leyland Finance, Hinduja Housing Finance, and OHM Mobility potentially requiring funds for growth plans, while Switch Mobility India is now profitable and in a comfortable position.

The Defense business is expected to continue its 20% growth trend for the next two to three years, supported by an order book exceeding INR1,500 crores, though execution spans multiple years.

Non-vehicle revenue momentum is expected to continue into Q1 FY27.

Q1 FY27 industry-level commercial vehicle performance is anticipated to be better than Q1 last year, driven by resilient baseline demand, fleet replacement, and GST benefits, despite current macroeconomic challenges.

For Q1 FY27, significant commodity cost increases, particularly in steel, pose a challenge to margins, which the company plans to mitigate through a 1-1.5% price increase and cost control measures, though sustaining the price increase for the full quarter is uncertain.

New product launches are expected to positively impact market share in the tipper and tractor trailer segments starting Q2 FY27.

Management anticipates some moderation in LCV and ICV demand from Q4 FY26 levels, but a relative pickup in heavy-duty trucks, which is favorable for the product mix.

**ALKEM LABORATORIES – NOMURA REPORT HIGHLIGHTS**  

**Brokerage View**
- Buy rating maintained
- Target price: ₹6,890

**Q4FY26 Performance**
- Revenue was in line with estimates
- EBITDA beat estimates by 11%
- Gross margin improved to 65.4%

- ₹603 Mn impact from changes in the Labor Code

Exxon Mobil Senior Vice President Neil Chapman warns that global crude inventories could fall to critically low levels within weeks, potentially triggering a sharp spike in physical oil prices.

reports.text:
GS on BDL
Sell, TP cut to Rs 1260

Execution remains a concern

Co missed expected execution for seventh year in a row

Channel checks suggest that miss in FY27 was due to:
1) domestic supply chain issues; and

2) unavailability of a component from an overseas supplier.
As a result, contrary to other defense peers under coverage, FY26 revenue declined 27% YoY.

Furthermore, inventory surged 75% YoY at INR 46.3bn (>2x FY26 revenue) mainly due to finished goods waiting for delivery.

On a positive front, estimate FY26 order book at INR 257bn, implying >10x book/bill (best among cos under coverage)

Order inflow in FY27 is likely to stay strong at INR 140bn, however, pick up in execution would be a key monitorable.

**APAR INDUSTRIES – NOMURA REPORT HIGHLIGHTS**  

**Brokerage View**
- Buy rating maintained
- Target price raised to ₹14,240

**Q4FY26 Performance**
- Strong Q4FY26 performance

- EBITDA beat consensus estimates by 5%

**Conductor Business**
- Conductor EBITDA/MT grew 8% YoY in Q4FY26
- Demand remains structurally healthy

**Cable Business**
- Robust growth continues in cables
- DC cables offer premium pricing opportunities
- Strong demand outlook for DC cable segment

**Capex Plans**
- FY27 capex guidance at ₹15 Bn
- Investments aimed at supporting future growth

**Earnings Outlook**
- FY27F EBITDA estimate raised by 7%
- FY28F EBITDA estimate raised by 9%
- EBITDA CAGR of 19% expected over FY26–FY29F

**Valuation**
- Trades at 37x FY27F EPS
- Trades at 29x FY28F EPS

**Investment View**
- Outlook remains on firm footing
- Strong demand across key business segments supports growth visibility

JPM on Ashok Leyland
Neutral, TP Rs 175

4Q a slight miss on JPMe while delivering a small beat on consensus expectations.

EBITDA at Rs 20.7bn (+15% YoY) was 2% below JPMe mainly due to higher staff and other expenses.

Management refrained from providing explicit growth and margin guidance for FY27.

They believe that underlying demand drivers remain robust but uncertainty around fuel price hikes as well as disruptions could lead to demand dislocation in the near term.

Management also emphasized that pricing discipline across the industry is expected to continue, with companies likely to implement multiple price increases during the year to offset rising commodity costs.

reports.text:
CLSA on Ashok Leyland
O-P, TP cut to Rs 183

4Q Ebitda margin came in at 14.6%, in line with consensus.
Its gross margin improved 82bps QoQ despite an increase in raw material costs, supported by a c.1% price hike in Jan and value re-engineering initiatives.
AL highlighted raw material costs remain on an inflationary trajectory in 1Q.

To offset this, AL implemented c.1.5% price increases and expects further mitigation through continuous value re-engineering.

On M&HCV goods volume growth, AL remains cautiously optimistic and expects GST rate cuts and replacement demand to continue supporting growth.

reports.text:
CITI on Ashok Leyland
Buy, TP Rs 206

4Q slightly above estimates as gross margin surprised positively (partly offset by lower operating leverage).

Mgmt. noted that underlying demand has been resilient despite recent diesel price hikes and unavailability of diesel in some pockets.

Even if there is some moderation in demand in 2Q, volumes could spring back in 2HFY27, driven by pent-up demand.

Even in exports market, retail demand has been steady, though dispatches have been impacted by logistics disruption
Structural growth drivers for CV demand (price cuts+ replacement of ageing fleet) are intact.

That said, do acknowledge commodity cost inflation, and some moderation in demand, resulting in minor downside revision of our FY27/28E EBITDA estimates.

reports.text:
CITI On GMR Airports

Reported MarQ-26 core profit of Rs1.3bn, vs -Rs2.5bn in MarQ-25 and Rs3.2bn in DecQ-25.

This is 18% lower than consensus where FY26 made 125% of Street’s FY26E estimate Rs3bn NPAT included one-offs such as cRs1bn claim received by Crete Airport and Rs1.2bn reverse of Hyderabad’s tax liability.

MarQ-26 revenue and EBITDA were -1% and -9% below Street, respectively.

Passenger traffic at 3 key airports was -1% QoQ/+1% YoY (Intl and Domestic -2%/+2% YoY).

Combined non-aero revenue saw +6% YoY (Delhi +4%, Hyderabad +14%, Goa +28%) as average spending per pax +5% YoY.

Delhi’s profits -47% QoQ to Rs1.2bn (non-aero per pax -6% QoQ), Hyderabad saw profits +108% QoQ to Rs1.8bn (non-aero per pax flat QoQ), while Goa saw losses narrow 15% QoQ to -Rs0.6bn.

Net debt stood at Rs340bn as of Mar26, compared to Rs345bn in Dec25.

RELAXO FOOTWEARS Q4 : ST NET PROFIT UP 20.4% AT 67.67 CR (YOY), UP 154.9% (QOQ). GOOD ✅ (On 1st Look)

REVENUE UP 8.0% AT 751.10 CR (YOY), UP 12.4% (QOQ)

EBITDA UP 10.6% AT 123.98 CR (YOY), UP 78.7% (QOQ)

reports.text:
GOODYEAR INDIA Q4 : ST NET PROFIT UP 98.8% AT 9.69 CR (YOY), DOWN 60.7% (QOQ). GOOD 🟢 (On 1st Look)

REVENUE UP 2.3% AT 616.28 CR (YOY), UP 1.5% (QOQ)

EBITDA UP 139.3% AT 41.49 CR (YOY), DOWN 6.0% (QOQ)

MARGINS 6.73% V 2.88% (YOY), 7.27% (QOQ)

⚠️ PAT of 9.69 Cr is entirely supported by exceptional gain of 19.83 Cr — without it, the business reported a pre-tax loss of 6.50 Cr. Core operations are loss-making this quarter despite decent EBITDA.

Strong EBITDA improvement YOY from 17.34 Cr to 41.49 Cr on nearly flat revenue is driven by sharp reduction in other expenses from 9,676L to 10,098L and better cost management — tyre business margins recovering meaningfully.
reports.channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

reports.text:
ASIAN HOTELS (NORTH) Q4 : CONS NET PROFIT DOWN 82.0% AT 31.30 CR (YOY), PROFIT VS LOSS (QOQ). WEAK 🔴 (On 1st Look)

REVENUE UP 12.5% AT 102.91 CR (YOY), UP 13.1% (QOQ)

EBITDA DOWN 19.6% AT 23.48 CR (YOY), UP 145.1% (QOQ)

MARGINS 22.82% V 31.92% (YOY), 10.53% (QOQ)

⚠️ PAT figures across all three quarters are heavily distorted by large exceptional items — Q4FY25 had an exceptional loss of 184.67 Cr making PAT comparison meaningless.

 EBITDA is the more reliable metric here.

Revenue recovery is encouraging — up 12.5% YOY and 13.1% QOQ at Hyatt Regency Delhi, showing strong hospitality demand recovery with RevPAR improvement.

EBITDA margin compression from 31.92% to 22.82% YOY is the real concern — finance costs remain elevated at 1,409.71L though declining sharply from 3,263.36L in Q4FY25, suggesting debt reduction underway.

reports.text:
Stocks in Action:

Bayer CropScience Ltd: Bayer CropScience delivered a resilient FY26 performance with full-year profitability due to their diversified portfolio despite a softer season for corn. Revenue from operations grew 3.7% to Rs. 5,675 crore for the full year, while Profit Before Tax rose to Rs. 855 crore from Rs. 707 crore in FY25. Full-year net profit grew 21% to Rs. 689 crore, supported by disciplined cost management and strong cash flow generation. In Q4, revenue reached Rs. 1,101 crore with PBT at Rs. 206 crore, reflecting a sequential recovery from a weaker Q3. Management emphasized a deliberate focus on long-term value creation over short-term volume acceleration, with the balance sheet remaining debt-free and cash-generative. Positive on Bayer CropScience given profitability trajectory, a strong parent-backed portfolio of differentiated crop protection and seeds products, and meaningful upside optionality from a normal monsoon cycle in FY27. 

Transport Corporation of India Ltd (TCI): TCI delivered a steady Q4FY26 performance, with consistent volume-driven growth across its multimodal logistics platform despite margin pressure from a competitive freight environment. Revenue grew 12.2% YoY to Rs. 1,324 crore, EBITDA rose 16.3% YoY to Rs. 142 crore, and PAT increased 8.7% YoY to Rs. 124 crore. The Supply Chain division remained the standout, delivering 14% revenue growth in FY26 driven by new contract wins and expansion in existing accounts across auto, FMCG and consumer durables sectors. Full-year consolidated revenue stood at Rs. 4,917 crore with PAT at Rs. 460 crore, reflecting uninterrupted profitable growth. Positive on TCI given steady compounding, multimodal diversification and strong promoter ownership.

*Rainfall Trading Arrives in India: A New Era of Weather Risk Management Begins Today* 

India's first rainfall derivatives contract starts trading today, marking a significant step in the evolution of the country's commodity and risk-management markets. Launched by the National Commodities and Derivatives Exchange (NCDEX), the product allows participants to hedge against excess or deficient rainfall. The initial contract is linked to Mumbai rainfall data and is designed to help businesses exposed to weather-related risks. Settlement is based on actual rainfall recorded during the contract period, creating a transparent mechanism for managing monsoon uncertainty.

The new instrument could benefit sectors such as agriculture, insurance, logistics, retail, tourism and infrastructure, where revenues are often affected by weather conditions. Supporters believe it will improve risk management and bring greater financial innovation to India’s markets. However, challenges remain, including limited awareness, low liquidity in the initial phase, and the need for wider participation from corporates and institutions. If successful, rainfall derivatives could become an important tool for managing climate-related financial risks in India.

**SATIN CREDITCARE NETWORK – STRONG RECOVERY & RETURN RATIO IMPROVEMENT**  

**Brokerage View**
- Mirae Asset Sharekhan maintained POSITIVE view
- Target price revised to ₹260
- Implies 16% upside from CMP ₹223
- Stock trades at attractive 0.6x FY28E book value

**AUM Growth Momentum**
- Consolidated AUM grew 18.7% YoY to ₹15,174 Cr
- Disbursements surged 42.8% YoY
- MSME business grew 59.4% YoY
- Housing finance business increased 38% YoY

**Profitability Recovery**
- Net profit jumped to ₹162 Cr from ₹22 Cr YoY
- PPOP surged 126.7% YoY to ₹290 Cr
- Strong growth supported by lower opex and better asset quality
- RoA improved sharply to 4.27%

**Asset Quality Improvement**
- GNPA declined 60 bps YoY to 3.10%
- NNPA reduced 140 bps YoY to 0.90%
- Credit cost improved to 2.06%
- Collection efficiencies improved across buckets

**Margin & Income Trends**
- NIM declined to 6.6% due to higher foreign borrowing costs
- Other income surged to ₹291 Cr
- Opex/AUM improved by 60 bps to 6.6%
- Stable NIM guidance of 12–13% going forward

**Subsidiary Growth**
- Satin Finserv AUM grew 92.5% YoY
- Satin Housing Finance reached ₹1,267 Cr AUM
- Housing finance business delivering strong traction
- Diversification beyond microfinance strengthening

**FY27 Guidance**
- Consolidated AUM growth guided at 25–30%
- Standalone AUM expected at ₹14,800–15,100 Cr
- Credit cost guided at 3–3.5%
- RoA expected to improve above 3%

**Long-Term Targets**
- Consolidated AUM target revised to ₹32,000 Cr by 2030
- Non-MFI business targeted at 30% of total AUM
- FY26–FY28 PAT CAGR estimated at 36%
- Average RoE expected around 16.7% by FY28

**Valuation Snapshot**
- FY28E PAT estimated at ₹614 Cr
- FY28E EPS estimated at ₹55.8
- FY28E RoA projected at 3.1%
- FY28E RoE projected at 16.7%

**Key Risks**
- Forex exposure from global borrowings
- Regulatory risks in microfinance segment
- Rupee depreciation impacting borrowing costs
- Sector-wide asset quality volatility

Source: Mirae Asset Sharekhan

**YES SECURITIES – US EARNINGS & MARKET UPDATE**  

**US Market Outlook**
- S&P 500 earnings momentum remains exceptionally resilient
- CY2026 EPS estimates upgraded from USD 296 to USD 339 over the past year
- CY2026 earnings revision trend at +10%, second-best upgrade cycle in 8 years
- Earnings fundamentals continue validating market rally beyond valuation expansion

**Mega-Cap Technology Trends**
- Magnificent 7 continue leading US earnings growth
- Aggregate revenue growth for mega caps at ~30% versus mid-single digits for broader market
- Nvidia remains standout performer with triple-digit revenue and EBIT growth
- Alphabet, Amazon, Meta and Microsoft continue posting superior profitability trends
- CY27 EPS growth for Magnificent 7 expected to moderate toward ~15%, converging with broader S&P 500 growth

**Sectoral Earnings Rotation**
- Industrials, Technology and Energy witnessing strongest earnings upgrades for CY2027
- Positive revisions seen in Dow, Intel, Seagate, Chevron and Devon Energy
- Signals improving cyclical recovery and capex-led expansion
- Energy and IT sectors show highest proportion of earnings upgrades

**Weakness & Downgrades**
- Consumer Discretionary and Communication Services witnessing maximum downgrade pressure
- Rate-sensitive and consumer-facing businesses remain under pressure
- Real estate-linked companies, cruise operators and media businesses seeing estimate cuts

**Market Breadth & Strategy View**
- Market leadership still concentrated in mega-cap technology
- Earnings breadth gradually improving beyond AI-led rally
- Selective cyclicals appear attractive due to improving estimate momentum
- Industrials, Energy and semiconductor-linked businesses remain preferred themes

**Key Data Points**
- S&P 500 CY27 earnings upgrades:
  - Magnificent 7: +4.4%
  - S&P 500 overall: +3.1%
  - S&P 500 excluding Mag 7: +1.4%
- S&P 500 Mar’26 earnings growth stood at 13.0% YoY
- EBIT growth for Mar’26 quarter at 12.6% YoY
- Revenue growth improved steadily to 7.5% YoY in Mar’26

Source: YES Securities Report

DEEPAK FERTILISERS – Q4FY26 & FY26 UPDATE  

Financial Performance
- FY26 revenue increased 12% YoY
- FY26 revenue reached ₹11,506 Cr
- Q4 revenue stood at ₹3,011 Cr
- FY26 EBITDA declined 13% YoY
- Q4 EBITDA declined 26% YoY
- Q4 PAT declined 50% YoY
- Adjusted FY26 PAT declined 18%

Key Margin Impacts
- Raw material inflation impacted margins
- Inadequate subsidy support impacted profitability
- Ammonia plant maintenance affected Q4
- One-off maintenance cost impacted EBITDA
- Lower finance costs partly offset pressures

Strategic Mix Improvement
- Specialty products contributed 33% revenue
- Mining Chemicals B2C contribution reached 16%
- Focus shifting towards solutions-led portfolio
- Premiumisation strategy improving earnings quality

Mining Chemicals
- Q4 volumes increased 12% YoY
- FY26 sales volumes grew 11%
- B2C volumes increased 21% YoY
- Q1FY27 demand expected steady
- Elevated FGAN prices supporting margins
- Supply disruptions supporting pricing discipline

Pharma & Specialty Chemicals
- Nitric Acid segment grew 11% YoY
- IPA volumes increased 22% YoY
- Domestic IPA demand showing recovery
- SGNA approvals received from customers
- Cororid and PuroGuard+ gained traction
- Products adopted across 246 hospitals

Crop Nutrition Business
- Fertiliser business faced weak Q4
- Input cost inflation impacted profitability
- Croptek sales reached 65 KT
- Specialty contribution improved to 33%
- Continued premiumisation across portfolio

Outlook
- Nitric Acid demand expected strengthening
- Limited imports supporting pricing environment
- IPA raw material availability remains constrained
- Focus shifting towards irrigated regions
- B2C expansion to support pricing discipline
- Differentiated products to sustain premium positioning

**LG ELECTRONICS INDIA – EXPORTS & PREMIUMISATION DRIVING GROWTH**  

**Brokerage View**
- YES Securities reiterated BUY rating
- Target price set at ₹1,776
- Implies 19.2% upside from CMP ₹1,489
- Valuation based on 45x FY28 EPS

**Growth Outlook**
- Management guiding mid-teen revenue growth for FY27
- Exports expected to become key growth driver
- Premiumisation increasing average selling prices
- Revenue/EBITDA/PAT CAGR estimated at 14%/25%/26% over FY26–FY28

**Export Expansion**
- Large-capacity refrigerator exports started in Q1FY27
- Presence expanding across 22 countries
- Side-by-side refrigerators gaining traction globally
- Export business delivering superior margins and FX hedge

**Home Appliances Performance**
- Segment revenue grew 5.7% YoY in Q4FY26
- Premium washing machines and refrigerators saw healthy demand
- Dishwashers emerged as a strong growth category
- EBIT margin stood at 11.9%

**Home Entertainment Strength**
- Segment revenue increased 19.6% YoY
- Large-screen television demand remained strong
- OLED TV market share maintained at 60%
- EBIT margin came at 13.4%

**Premium Product Momentum**
- Strong traction in 5-star ACs and French-door refrigerators
- Essential Series gaining market acceptance
- New product launches across premium categories underway
- Cricket World Cup boosted premium TV demand

**Localization & Manufacturing**
- Localization improved to 55.2% in FY26
- Targeting annual localization improvement of 1–2%
- Sri City plant investment of ₹50 Bn progressing
- Phased commissioning expected from late CY26

**Pricing & Margins**
- Company implemented price hikes to protect margins
- Additional price hikes planned from July onwards
- Commodity inflation and INR depreciation impacted profitability
- Margins expected to remain in low double digits

**Q4FY26 Performance**
- Revenue grew 8.1% YoY to ₹80,537 Mn
- EBITDA stood at ₹9,455 Mn
- EBITDA margin at 11.7%
- PAT came at ₹6,928 Mn

**Balance Sheet & Cash Position**
- Cash balance stood at ₹44.76 Bn
- Working capital at ₹22.73 Bn
- Debt-free balance sheet maintained
- FY26 capex stood at ₹5.5 Bn

**Future Triggers**
- Export growth expected to cushion domestic slowdown
- RAC demand improved during April and May
- Higher localization to support margins
- Sri City expansion to aid future capacity growth

Source: YES Securities

Rikhav Securities Limited Reports EBITDA of ₹32.53 Cr & PAT of ₹18.96 Cr in FY26

Rikhav Securities Limited, one of the leading diversified stock market services providers has announced its Audited Financial Results for H2 & FY26.

H2 FY26 Financial Highlights
* Total Income of ₹ 1604.95 Cr
* EBITDA of ₹ 6.57 Cr
* PAT of ₹ 1.20 Cr
* EPS of ₹ 0.31

FY26 Financial Highlights
* Total Income of ₹ 1991.62 Cr
* EBITDA of ₹ 32.53 Cr
* PAT of ₹ 18.96 Cr
* EPS of ₹ 4.95

Financial & Business Updates FY26
- Invested ₹5.40 Cr in technology infrastructure and software during FY26, leading to higher depreciation
- Profitability impacted by ₹22.93 Cr loss from share trading activities
- Unrealised SLBM profit of ₹4.33 Cr not recognised in FY26 financials and expected to be realised in upcoming quarter
- Current liabilities increased mainly due to SLBM borrowings of ₹47.34 Cr

Top fund managers have very different takes on Indian IT.

Rajeev Thakkar is finding value at current levels and is buying IT across funds.

Prashant Jain is neutral. His view the sector may need 2 more years for better clarity.

Kenneth Andrade has completely exited IT, citing uncertainty around the real impact of AI on the sector.

S. Naren, the contrarian, is also seeing value and continues to hold IT stocks.

Samir Arora is bearish on traditional IT, with zero allocation there, but prefers select tech and capital market plays.

Sunil Singhania has no exposure to traditional IT stocks, but is buying a product-based tech company.

It will be interesting to see who gets this right.

Indian Railways’ ₹40,000Cr Wagon Tender Signals Multi-Year Demand Visibility

Indian Railways plans ₹40,000Cr freight wagon procurement over 3–4 years.

Tender size targets nearly 100,000 wagons, exceeding the 2022 procurement cycle.

Annual procurement could reach 35,000–40,000 wagons under phased ordering structure.

First tranche of orders likely during July–September quarter this fiscal year.

Initiative aims to absorb expanded manufacturing capacities across domestic wagon makers.

Previous 2022 tender worth ₹32,000Cr also covered roughly 100,000 wagons.

Pace Digitek is building FY27–FY28 growth visibility around BESS-led manufacturing scale-up. ⚡

The company plans to expand BESS capacity from 2.5 GWh to 10 GWh in phases starting from Q2/Q3FY27, with new facilities and in-house container fabrication aimed at improving cost efficiency, delivery timelines and margin profile.

Order book stands at ₹11,338 cr, diversified across BESS, telecom, OFC and digital infrastructure projects.

Revenue guidance:
FY27E: ₹3,200–3,400 cr
FY28E: ₹4,000–4,200 cr
FY26: ₹2641 Cr

The opportunity is large, but the real monitor will be execution — timely commissioning, order book conversion and margin protection while scaling.

NIS Management Limited Secures Multiple Work Orders Worth ₹2.32 Cr

NIS Management Limited has announced the receipt of multiple work orders from reputed commercial and government clients.

Work Order Details

1. Client: Nesco Limited
Order – Facility Supervisors & Attendants Deployment
* Scope: Deployment of Facility Supervisors and Attendants at Nesco Complex
* Order Value : ₹1.48 Cr
* Execution Period: Up to March 31, 2027
Order – Facility Attendants Deployment
* Scope: Deployment of Facility Attendants
* Order Value: ₹46.88 Lakh
* Execution Period: Up to March 31, 2027

2. Client: Public Works Department (PWD), Kolkata Government of West Bengal
Order – Housekeeping Personnel Engagement
* Scope : Engagement of housekeeping personnel at the New Secretariat Building, Block-B, Administrative Block of High Court
* Order Value : ₹36.71 Lakh
* Execution Period: 365 days from the date of commencement

The work order received from PWD, Kolkata, Government of West Bengal, continues to remain operational and on track for execution post the recent state elections in West Bengal.

These newly secured work orders from both public and private sector clients underscore the Company’s diversified execution capabilities and strong operational framework

Captain Polyplast Limited - Q4 & FY26 Concall Highlights

Company Overview
Captain Polyplast Limited has been a leading player in the Micro Irrigation Systems (MIS) industry for nearly three decades. The Company has strategically diversified into the Solar EPC segment, focusing on solar water pumping systems and rooftop solar solutions. Backed by a strong distribution network of over 750 dealers across key agricultural states, the Company provides integrated solutions along with robust after-sales support to farmers and customers.

Financial Performance

Q4 FY26 Performance
* Reported its highest-ever quarterly revenue of ₹142 Cr, registering an 80% YoY growth from ₹79.1 Cr in Q4 FY25.
* EBITDA increased 66% YoY to ₹14.16 Cr.
* EBITDA Margin stood at 9.96%.
* Net Profit surged 91% YoY to ₹9.76 Cr, while EPS stood at ₹1.64.

FY26 Performance
* Total Income reached ₹419 Cr, reflecting a robust 44% YoY growth over FY25.
* Full-year EBITDA stood at ₹46.32 Cr with an EBITDA Margin of 11.03%.

Revenue Mix & Profitability
Business Segment Mix
* Current revenue mix stands at approximately 80:20 / 85:15 (MIS : Solar EPC).
* Management aims to achieve a 50:50 revenue mix within the next two years.

Growing Non-Subsidy Contribution
* The cash (non-subsidy) segment contributed 15% of MIS revenues during FY26.
* The rooftop solar business operates entirely on a cash-and-carry model.

Profitability Outlook
Management highlighted that solar pump margins remain structurally aligned with MIS margins, indicating no margin dilution from the evolving business mix.

Operations & Expansion
Ahmedabad Facility & Backward Integration
* The new 70,000 sq. ft. Ahmedabad manufacturing facility is now operational.
* The facility manufactures high-margin micro irrigation components in-house.
* Manufacturing Capacity. Combined capacity across Rajkot, Kurnool, and Ahmedabad plants can support up to ₹400 Cr MIS revenue without any major additional capex.

Solar Pumps Business Momentum
* Executed 1,500 solar pumps in Maharashtra during the first year of operations.
* Secured a fresh 500-pump order worth ₹11 Cr.

Raw Material & Margin Trends
Q4 profitability was impacted by a sharp increase in raw material prices during March 2026, primarily due to unforeseen geopolitical developments. This remained the key reason behind the 86 bps margin contraction.

Management Guidance & Growth Outlook
* Management is targeting 20–25% CAGR growth in the MIS segment over the next three years, primarily driven by market share gains.
* The Solar EPC segment is expected to grow at a faster pace to gradually match MIS revenues.

Export Strategy
* Exports currently contribute around 5% of MIS revenue.
* Focus remains on drip irrigation products for African and Latin American markets.
* Management targets double-digit export contribution within the next five years.

Reduce, TP Rs 1100

4QFY26 consolidated EBITDA beat estimates across both copper and aluminum divisions.

CITI on Hindalco
Neutral, TP Rs 1170

At Rs101.8bn (6% ahead), 4Q consol EBITDA rose 6% yoy/28% qoq

QoQ increase is attributed to better performance across divisions.

India upstream: Rs54.5bn vs. Rs48.3bn in 3Q; India downstream: Rs2.6bn vs. Rs2.3bn; Copper: Rs9bn vs. Rs6bn; Novelis: Rs42bn vs. Rs31bn.

India upstream EBITDA/t was $1,756 vs. $1,572 in 3Q and should continue to rally (spot $3,650), offset partly by 5% higher costs

Est debt should peak at $8.2bn in FY27 & fall thereafter ($6.8bn as of Mar26); estimate net D/EBITDA at 1.6x vs. 1.8x in Mar26
Global team is structurally bullish aluminium; CY27 estimate is $3,800

Maintain Neutral, given LME upside from spot may not be significant

Potential upsides such as (1) Bay Minette profitability, (2) higher India smelting capacity, and (3) captive coal are back-ended.

Downgrade to hold, TP raised Rs 1490

It expects US$2bn revenue from the US in two years (vs. US$1.6bn in FY26), double-digit growth in Europe in FY27, and 21% consolidated margin in FY27

While it expects new launches, portfolio expansion and acquisitions to lead overall growth, expect the full impact of these initiatives only from FY28

BoFA Sec on Aurobindo
Buy, TP Rs 1700

While ARBP’s US sales were 8% lower QoQ (vs BofAe flattish) on soft seasonality, company reported an inline EBITDA of Rs17.5Bn (20% margin).

This was supported by the 160bps QoQ gross margin expansion largely on Pen-G plant ramp-up.

While $2Bn US revenue run-rate (vs $1.6Bn in FY26) depends on Lannett deal closure (2QFY27), 21% margin guidance is 50-100bps higher than street/BofA estimates.

@Stocksip

GS on Aurobindo
Buy, TP Rs 1550

4Q broadly in line with estimates (+2% vs. GSe), with sales/EBITDA growing by +6%/+1% yoy driven by strong growth in Europe (+29% yoy) and growth markets (+25% yoy) partially offset by a slower US which is still seeing impacts of the gRevlimid ramp-down (-13% yoy).

EBITDA margin came in steady at 20.3% (-16bps vs. GSe), as higher GMs (+216bps yoy) were offset by higher employee costs

Co aspires to achieve high single digit topline growth (ex-gRevlimid) while guiding to maintain margins at 21%+ mark in FY27
